skill.name: specdd-risk
skill.description: Classify risk level before starting SpecDD work on a project. Use this skill to identify write authority, stop conditions, and concrete risk factors.
skill.summary: Classify risk before starting SpecDD development work on a project.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 7 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' and 2 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns. After evaluation, all 9 are false positives: the backticks are markdown inline code formatting for file paths and skill names (e.g., `.specdd/bootstrap.md`, `specdd-adopt`), and the 'weak cryptographic' findings at lines 3 and 60 are actually the skill description and a documentation line about destructive operations. The file is a benign skill specification document with no executable code.

low_findings.title: Markdown backtick usage flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer detected 7 instances of backtick characters and flagged them as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. All instances are false positives — they are standard markdown inline code formatting used to reference file paths (e.g., `.specdd/bootstrap.md`) and skill names (e.g., `specdd-adopt`). No code execution occurs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct inspection of the file confirms all backticks are markdown inline code formatting for documentation references, not shell or Ruby execution constructs.

content.user_title: Classify Project Risk Before SpecDD Work
content.value_statement: Starting development work without assessing risk leads to unauthorized changes and contract violations. This skill provides a structured risk classification workflow for SpecDD projects before any edits begin.
actual_capabilities: Classify risk as low, medium, or high with concrete reasons before any development work
Resolve the active bootstrap chain from .specdd bootstrap files in the correct order
Identify write authority and stop conditions for a target file or change
Check affected public contracts, security boundaries, data shape, migrations, and dependencies
Determine the smallest safe next step and required verification before work is complete
limitations: Only applicable to projects that already have a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file
Does not edit files during risk assessment unless explicitly asked by the user
Does not perform the actual code changes — it classifies risk only
Requires the active bootstrap rules to be reliable in context or available to read

use_cases.title: Pre-work risk gate for a pull request
use_cases.description: Use before opening a pull request to confirm the change has clear write authority and does not cross spec boundaries.
use_cases.target_user: Backend engineers preparing changes to a SpecDD project

use_cases.title: Security boundary review for API changes
use_cases.description: Apply when a change touches public APIs, auth, secrets, payments, or persistent data to classify and document the risk level.

use_cases.title: Onboarding risk assessment for new contributors
use_cases.description: Walk a new contributor through the risk classification process before they make their first edit to a SpecDD project.

prompt_templates.title: Basic risk classification
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the specdd-risk skill to classify the risk of the following change: {describe the change}. Identify write authority, stop conditions, and the risk level.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time use — classify a simple change before starting work

prompt_templates.title: API change risk assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the specdd-risk skill to assess the risk of modifying a public API endpoint that handles authentication tokens. Check affected public contracts, security boundaries, and verification needs.
prompt_templates.scenario: Assessing risk for changes to public APIs or auth systems

prompt_templates.title: Migration risk evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the specdd-risk skill to evaluate the risk of a database migration that changes the schema for the users table. Determine if destructive operations or irreversible state changes are involved.
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluating risk for database migrations or schema changes

prompt_templates.title: Multi-spec change coordination
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the specdd-risk skill to resolve the effective spec chain for a change that touches both the billing service and the notification service. Identify cross-spec boundaries and ownership changes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced — coordinating changes that cross multiple spec boundaries

output_examples.input: I need to add a new field to the User model and update the API response.
output: Write authority: .specdd/bootstrap.md → .specdd/bootstrap.project.md grants write authority to /src/models/user.py
Stop conditions: No live deployments during the change window
Affected contracts: Public API response shape (breaking change for clients expecting strict schema)
Risk factors: Touches persistent data and public API contract
Next step: Update the API spec first, then apply the schema migration with a rollback plan
Verification: Integration tests for the new field and backward compatibility tests for existing clients

output_examples.input: I want to update the CSS color of the login button.
output: Write authority: .specdd/bootstrap.md grants UI write authority without further restrictions
Affected contracts: None — visual change only
Next step: Apply the change directly
Verification: Visual review in the staging environment

best_practices: Always resolve the bootstrap chain before classifying risk to ensure the correct project rules apply
Identify write authority explicitly before proposing any edits
Document the concrete reasons for the risk level, not just the level itself
End every risk assessment with the smallest safe next step and required verification
anti_patterns: Starting edits before identifying write authority and stop conditions
Rereading the entire spec chain when only one governing spec is needed for the current decision
Inferring authority from similar filenames or conventions instead of the active bootstrap rules
Skipping risk classification because the change seems small or obvious

faq.question: What is SpecDD?
faq.answer: SpecDD stands for Spec-Driven Development, a methodology where specifications drive the development process and serve as the source of truth for project contracts.

faq.question: Does this skill edit files?
faq.answer: No. The skill classifies risk only. It will not edit files during assessment unless you explicitly ask it to.

faq.question: What if my project does not have a .specdd/bootstrap.md?
faq.answer: This skill will not proceed. Use the specdd-adopt skill instead to add or adopt SpecDD in a new or existing non-SpecDD project.

faq.question: What risk levels does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill classifies risk as low, medium, or high based on the change's impact on public contracts, security, data, and dependencies.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: How do I know the smallest safe next step?
faq.answer: The skill always ends with a recommended next step and the verification needed before the work is considered complete.
